name: Cerro Jabalcón – circular trail running from Zújar with views of the Negratín Reservoir
name_original: Cerro Jabalcón – trail running circular desde Zújar con vistas al Embalse de Negratín
region_intro: Jogging around Zújar offers diverse terrain, from the prominent Cerro Jabalcón mountain to the shores of the vast Negratín Reservoir. The region is characterized by unique "badlands" geological formations and fertile valleys, providing varied running routes. Pine forests on Jabalcón Mountain offer shaded paths, while the Carramaiza flatlands present gentler options. This varied landscape ensures a range of running experiences for different preferences. Best jogging routes around Zújar The most popular jogging route is Cerro Jabalcón – circular trail running from Zújar with views of the Negratín Reservoir, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the reservoir and surrounding landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Enchanted Forest of Las Higueras Trail, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This running route leads through a unique forest environment, providing a different scenic backdrop. Local runners also love the Historic Center of Baza loop from Baza, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through the historic center of Baza, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Jogging around Zújar is defined by mountainous inclines of Cerro Jabalcón, reservoir-side paths along Negratín, and unique badlands formations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate runs to more difficult trails. The routes in Zújar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Zújar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Zújar?
A: Zújar offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 45 trails available for exploration. These range from easy paths to more challenging runs, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Zújar?
A: Jogging in Zújar provides a diverse range of terrains. You can expect mountainous inclines around &lt;strong&gt;Cerro Jabalcón&lt;/strong&gt;, scenic paths along the shores of the &lt;strong&gt;Negratín Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, and unique 'badlands' geological formations. There are also shaded pine forests and flatter agricultural areas like the Carramaiza flatlands for gentler runs.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Zújar?
A: Yes, Zújar has several running options suitable for beginners. While many routes are moderate, there are at least 2 easy trails available, perfect for those new to running or looking for a more relaxed pace.

Q: What are some of the more challenging running options in Zújar?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Zújar offers 5 difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31860064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedra del Baño loop from Embalse del Negratín&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 12.8 km with significant elevation changes. Another demanding trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Jabalcón loop from Cuesta del Raedor&lt;/a&gt;, spanning nearly 17.4 km.

Q: When is the best time of year to go jogging in Zújar?
A: Zújar's climate makes it suitable for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, especially on trails with less exposure.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the running routes?
A: Absolutely! Many running routes in Zújar offer breathtaking views. The slopes of &lt;strong&gt;Cerro Jabalcón&lt;/strong&gt; provide panoramic vistas of the surrounding region and the Negratín Reservoir. You can also find stunning perspectives from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narváez Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which is a highlight in the area.

Q: What natural attractions can I see near the jogging paths?
A: Beyond the stunning landscapes, you can encounter several natural attractions. The unique 'badlands' formations of the Granada Geopark are a highlight. You might also come across the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of Health&lt;/a&gt; or the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de la Heredad&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2793795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert Canyons&lt;/a&gt; are also a notable geological feature nearby.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Zújar?
A: Yes, Zújar features several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986582328/cerro-jabalcon-circular-trail-running-from-zujar-with-views-of-the-negratin-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Jabalcón – circular trail running from Zújar with views of the Negratín Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 12.5 km loop offering spectacular scenery.

Q: Is parking available near the running trails in Zújar?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, Zújar is generally well-equipped for visitors arriving by car. Many trailheads, especially those leading to popular areas like Cerro Jabalcón or the Negratín Reservoir, have designated parking areas or roadside spots suitable for leaving your vehicle.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging routes in Zújar?
A: Yes, Zújar offers routes that are suitable for families. Look for the 'easy' or 'moderate' difficulty trails, which often have gentler inclines and shorter distances. The old railway paths, part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6070444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde, Baza - Baúl&lt;/a&gt;, provide a relatively flat and scenic option that can be enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Zújar?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in Zújar, especially in natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them. Specific rules might apply in certain reserves, so checking local signage is advisable.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Zújar?
A: The running trails in Zújar are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an impressive average rating of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain and stunning natural beauty, particularly the views of the Negratín Reservoir and the unique geological formations.
tips: A good place to run at the end of the day, even if you have to go down with a front, the sunsets are spectacular.
Wonderful view of the Negratin reservoir.

Great geological interest is the so-called Piedra Horadada. also known as Horro and Ojo de la Heredad, a natural arch several meters high, formed in the limestone rock of the west face of Jabalcón and next to which this circular track passes, so there will be no difficulty in locating this impressive a place that may well deserve our photographic attention.

waypoints.name: Views from Horro Mano de Hierro
